column1: # ZAP Baseline Scan Rules — PassGuard
# Format: <rule-id>
# Actions:
#   FAIL  — alert blocks the pipeline (default for High/Medium)
#   WARN  — alert is logged but does not block
#   IGNORE — alert is suppressed entirely
# Rule IDs: https://www.zaproxy.org/docs/alerts/
# Philosophy: We FAIL on anything that represents a real exploitable issue in
# our API. We WARN or IGNORE on findings that are dev-environment artefacts
# or that do not apply to a stateless Bearer-token API.
# 10015: Incomplete or No Cache-control Header
# Reason to IGNORE: This is a JSON REST API with no browser-cached pages.
# Cache-control is meaningful for HTML/CDN scenarios, not API responses.
# ── Cross-domain JS source file inclusion ────────────────────────────────────
# 10017: Cross-Domain JavaScript Source File Inclusion
# Reason to IGNORE: We serve no JavaScript. ZAP fires this on any <script>
# tag in an error response. Not applicable to a pure JSON API.
# 10021: X-Content-Type-Options header missing
# Reason to WARN (not FAIL): Helmet sets this header. If ZAP still reports
# it, it may be a timing issue during startup. Track but don't block.
# 10096: Timestamp Disclosure
# Reason to IGNORE: Our /health endpoints intentionally return a timestamp
# field (ISO 8601) for monitoring. This is expected, not a disclosure.
# 10202: Absence of Anti-CSRF Tokens
# Reason to IGNORE: CSRF attacks require cookie-based session auth. PassGuard
# uses stateless Bearer tokens (HMAC-signed JWTs). A browser cannot silently
# attach an Authorization header — CSRF does not apply.
# Reference: OWASP CSRF Prevention Cheat Sheet §Token-based Mitigation
# ── Information disclosure via server banner ─────────────────────────────────
# 10036: Server Leaks Version Information via "Server" HTTP Response Header
# Reason to WARN: Helmet removes Express's "X-Powered-By" but uvicorn may
# still emit a Server header. Track and remove in production (WAF strip).
# 10038: Content Security Policy (CSP) Header Not Set
# Reason to WARN: CSP is critical for browser-facing apps. Our API only
# returns JSON — no HTML, no scripts. Warn so we remember to add it if we
# ever add a UI surface.
# 10049: Non-Storable Content
# Reason to IGNORE: ZAP flags JSON responses without Cache-control as
# potentially cacheable. In practice, API clients don't cache POST responses.
# Any alert NOT listed above uses ZAP's default action:
#   High   → FAIL
#   Medium → WARN
#   Low    → WARN
#   Info   → IGNORE
